message:
chore(beads): add ft-1i2ge.8.15 tx track E2E harness hardening issue

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -303,6 +303,7 @@
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.12","title":"[mission][H12] Chaos/performance hardening and tx rollout-readiness evidence","description":"## Objective
Harden tx subsystem with chaos and performance validation, then package rollout-ready go/no-go evidence.

## Scope
- Fault injection campaigns for backend outages, stale state storms, delayed receipts, and partial network partition semantics.
- Throughput/latency benchmarking for tx plan and tx run under swarm-scale load.
- Rollout criteria and residual-risk package for production decisioning.

## Deliverables
- Chaos report with failure taxonomy outcomes.
- Performance budget report with before/after evidence.
- Tx-specific go/no-go checklist contribution.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Chaos suite validates bounded failure behavior and absence of unsafe side-effect escalation.
2. Performance report includes explicit budgets and measured tx overhead at target scale.
3. Rollout package includes rollback readiness, kill-switch verification, and residual-risk ownership.
4. Heavy validation runs produce reproducible logs/artifacts suitable for audit and postmortem.
5. Tx readiness decision is evidence-based, not intuition-based.
6. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_chaos_perf.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_chaos_perf_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior. Required report artifact: `docs/metrics/mission_tx_rollout_readiness.json`.
7. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: do not ship tx subsystem without objective chaos/perf evidence and explicit residual-risk statements.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SwiftAnchor","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:35.462307Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-27T08:02:47.044093Z","closed_at":"2026-02-27T08:02:47.044076Z","close_reason":"Implemented test_mission_tx_chaos_perf.sh, wired ft_1i2ge_8_12 scenario, executed rch-backed chaos/perf suites, and generated mission_tx_rollout_readiness.json with structured evidence logs","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["chaos","e2e-tests","go-no-go","integration-tests","logging","mission","performance","quality-gate","rollout","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.12","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.6.7","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.12","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.6.8","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.12","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.12","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.11","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.13","title":"[ft-1i2ge.8][support] Add MCP tx tool behavior regressions","description":"Add bounded regression coverage in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs for MCP tx surfaces: verify show(include_contract) envelope shape and invalid-step error handling for tx_run/tx_rollback without touching the shell harnesses or dirty web/MCP seam files.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentWolf","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:46:07.723557Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-13T00:50:49.766339Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T00:50:49.763199Z","close_reason":"Added MCP tx tool behavior regressions in crates/frankenterm-core/src/mcp_tools.rs; rustfmt --check and git diff --check passed; targeted cargo test not run because rch workers probe --all failed with RCH-E100 on all 8 workers and repo policy forbids local fail-open cargo.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.13","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T00:46:07.723557Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.14","title":"[ft-1i2ge.8][support] Harden tx E2E scripts for fail-closed rch execution","description":"Update tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_8_5.sh, test_ft_1i2ge_8_6.sh, and test_ft_1i2ge_8_7.sh to use fail-closed rch execution helpers and structured diagnostics consistent with the newer mission tx harnesses, without permitting local cargo fallback.","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"SilentWolf","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:35:37.643194Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-13T02:40:06.450417Z","closed_at":"2026-03-13T02:40:06.449765Z","close_reason":"Hardened tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_8_5.sh, test_ft_1i2ge_8_6.sh, and test_ft_1i2ge_8_7.sh for fail-closed rch execution; bash -n, shellcheck, and git diff --check passed; rch doctor passed; rch workers probe --all still fails with RCH-E100 on all 8 workers so no remote cargo run was attempted.","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.14","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-13T02:35:37.643194Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
+{"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.15","title":"[ft-1i2ge.8][support] Harden tx track E2E harness for fail-closed rch execution","description":"Bounded support slice for tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_track.sh. Replace legacy local cargo/python fallback with fail-closed RCH preflight and remote-only execution semantics consistent with newer mission tx harnesses. Scope: this harness only, plus minimal documentation/comments if needed. Require structured diagnostics for rch check/probe/smoke and explicit local-fallback/stall detection so the tx track can gather policy-compliant evidence without silently compiling locally.","status":"in_progress","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"VioletWolf","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:39:21.738276Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-03-15T01:39:21.738276Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["e2e","mission","rch","testing","tx"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.15","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-03-15T01:39:21.738276Z","created_by":"jemanuel","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.2","title":"[mission][H2] Intent ledger and causal receipt persistence model","description":"## Objective
Implement a durable intent ledger that records every transaction decision and causal receipt needed for replay, audit, and incident response.

## Scope
- Ledger schema for intent, planned DAG, precondition results, prepare outcomes, commit receipts, compensation receipts, and final outcome.
- Append-only/event-sourced write discipline for forensic integrity.
- Query surface for `tx show` timeline reconstruction.
- Correlation hooks for mission run id, pane ids, agent ids, bead/thread ids, policy checks, reservation ids, approval ids.

## Deliverables
- Storage schema + migration plan.
- Ledger writer/reader abstractions.
- Causal receipt model with deterministic ordering and hash/chaining fields.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Ledger captures all mandatory tx lifecycle events and correlation IDs without lossy shortcuts.
2. `tx show` can reconstruct a full causal timeline from ledger-only data.
3. Write path is crash-safe and restart-safe (no orphaned half-events without detectable status).
4. Unit tests validate serialization/deserialization and ordering guarantees.
5. Integration tests validate persistence + recovery after simulated mid-transaction restart.
6. Logging includes ledger_write_result, schema_version, and event_hash for every append.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_ledger.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_ledger_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: treat ledger fidelity as non-negotiable; if it is incomplete, rollback and replay confidence collapse.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticGate","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:30.255224Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-24T16:11:01.722146Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T16:11:01.722133Z","close_reason":"done","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["audit","e2e-tests","integration-tests","ledger","logging","mission","quality-gate","storage","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.1.5","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.2","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1404,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RusticGate: Claimed. Starting implementation of durable intent ledger and causal receipt persistence model. Will build on BoldMeadow's TxIntent/TxPlan/TxReceipt/TxOutcome types from plan.rs.","created_at":"2026-02-24T16:02:42Z"},{"id":1405,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.2","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"RusticGate: Implementation complete. Added IntentLedger with SHA-256 hash-chained entries, LedgerRecorder for high-level lifecycle recording, 8 LedgerEntryKind variants (IntentRegistered, PlanCreated, PreconditionEvaluated, StateTransition, StepExecuted, CompensationExecuted, OutcomeSealed, ReceiptRecorded), LedgerCorrelation with mission/pane/agent/bead/thread/policy/reservation/approval hooks, query surfaces (by kind, time range, pane, agent, state timeline), JSONL persistence, tamper detection via hash chain validation. 30 unit tests all pass. E2E script at tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_ledger.sh. Commit a0103b48.","created_at":"2026-02-24T16:10:57Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","title":"[mission][H3] tx plan DAG compiler with precondition and compensation graph","description":"## Objective
Build `tx plan` compilation that turns candidate actions into an executable dependency graph with explicit preconditions and compensations.

## Scope
- Compile action DAG from mission candidates + operator input.
- Attach preconditions (policy, reservation, approval, target reachability, context freshness).
- Attach compensating action candidates per step.
- Produce deterministic plan hash and explainability payload (selected vs rejected edges/steps).

## Deliverables
- Planner compiler module and deterministic ordering rules.
- `tx plan` result envelope with DAG + preconditions + compensators + risk summary.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. Compiler emits a deterministic DAG and plan hash for identical inputs.
2. Every executable step has explicit preconditions and compensation metadata.
3. Rejected steps/edges include reason codes and explain payload.
4. Unit tests cover DAG validity, cycle rejection, and deterministic sorting/tie-break behavior.
5. Property tests validate no dangling dependencies and full compensation coverage assumptions.
6. Structured logs emit compile decisions, rejected reason codes, and plan_hash.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_plan_compiler.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_plan_compiler_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: plan-time quality determines runtime safety envelope; weak planning here leaks complexity into prepare/commit logic.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"RusticGate","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:30.813999Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-24T17:14:21.814797Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T17:14:21.503664Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["dag","e2e-tests","explainability","integration-tests","logging","mission","planner","property-tests","quality-gate","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.2.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.2.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.2.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.2.6","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1417,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"Created tx_plan_compiler.rs: compile_tx_plan() compiles planner assignments into DAG with topological sort, parallel levels, preconditions (5 kinds), compensating actions (5 kinds), risk classification (4 levels), FNV-1a deterministic hash, rejected edges tracking. 30 tests. Committed 5c084acd.","created_at":"2026-02-24T17:14:21Z"}]}
 {"id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","title":"[mission][H4] Prepare-phase coordinator (policy, reservation, approval, readiness)","description":"## Objective
Implement prepare-phase orchestration that validates all safety gates and acquires execution readiness before commit side effects.

## Scope
- Evaluate policy preflight, reservation feasibility/acquisition, approval requirements, target liveness, and stale-state guards.
- Prepare receipts for each step with deny/defer/ready outcomes.
- Abort early with structured rationale when prepare cannot satisfy invariants.

## Deliverables
- Prepare-phase coordinator + per-step readiness records.
- Clear mapping from prepare outcomes to commit eligibility.

## Acceptance Criteria
1. Implement the full scope described in this bead Objective/Details/Goals with no functional regressions in adjacent behavior.
2. Add comprehensive unit tests covering happy-path behavior, edge/boundary conditions, and explicit error/failure paths for the touched logic.
3. Add integration/E2E scenario scripts that validate real workflow behavior end-to-end, including at least one failure-injection path and one recovery path.
4. Ensure E2E scenarios emit detailed structured logs (timestamp, component, correlation_id, scenario_id, input, decision/outcome, reason_code/error_code) and retain artifacts for triage.
5. Validate determinism and policy/safety constraints for this change, including repeat-run stability and contract-level assertions on touched interfaces.
6. Wire new/updated tests into the project test workflow and document exact execution commands/log locations in implementation notes.

## User Impact
1. Specify the concrete operator/agent workflow pain this bead removes (speed, reliability, safety, or clarity).
2. Define measurable user-facing outcomes (for example p95 latency, failure/timeout rate, time-to-recovery, or reduction in manual intervention steps).
3. Define no-regression UX guarantees and explicit rollback/degradation behavior if those outcomes are not met.","acceptance_criteria":"1. No commit-side effects occur unless all required prepare checks pass for the commit set.
2. Prepare denials and conflicts map to stable reason codes and remediation hints.
3. Reservation/policy/approval evidence is attached to prepare receipts.
4. Unit tests cover allow/deny/timeout/conflict permutations.
5. Integration tests validate reservation conflict and approval-expiry behavior.
6. Logs include prepare_outcome per step with policy/reservation/approval correlation IDs.
7. Deliverables must include comprehensive unit tests plus executable e2e script `tests/e2e/test_mission_tx_prepare.sh` and detailed structured logs under `tests/e2e/logs/mission_tx_prepare_*.jsonl` proving nominal, edge, and failure-path behavior.
8. Structured log contract for this bead requires fields: timestamp, component, scenario_id, correlation_id, decision_path, input_summary, outcome, reason_code, error_code, artifact_path.","notes":"Future-self note: prepare is a hard boundary, not an advisory hint; failing closed is preferable to unsafe partial commit.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ChartreuseCliff","created_at":"2026-02-22T23:29:31.308921Z","created_by":"jemanuel","updated_at":"2026-02-24T21:53:48.356885Z","closed_at":"2026-02-24T21:53:48.356829Z","source_repo":".","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"labels":["approvals","e2e-tests","integration-tests","logging","mission","policy","quality-gate","reservations","safety","test-coverage","test-logging","transactional-execution","tx","unit-tests","user-impact"],"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.4.1","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.4.2","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.4.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.4.4","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""},{"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","depends_on_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.3","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-02-23T00:14:26Z","created_by":"import","metadata":"{}","thread_id":""}],"comments":[{"id":1449,"issue_id":"ft-1i2ge.8.4","author":"Jeff Emanuel","text":"ChartreuseCliff: Implemented prepare-phase coordinator in plan.rs. Added types: TxPrepareStepReadiness (Ready/Denied/Deferred), TxPrepareStepReceipt (per-step evaluation), TxPrepareOutcome (AllReady/Denied/Deferred), TxPrepareReport (aggregate with canonical_string), TxPrepareGateInput (caller-provided safety gate results). Added evaluate_prepare_phase() pure function: evaluates kill-switch, then per-step policy->reservation->approval->liveness gates in priority order. Kill-switch active = all steps denied early. Policy deny = terminal. Reservation/approval/liveness = deferrable. Denied trumps deferred. Missing gate = deferred. 17 unit tests + E2E harness tests/e2e/test_ft_1i2ge_8_4.sh.","created_at":"2026-02-24T21:53:44Z"}]}
